column24: Comments

column2: Emma Frost Corrupts the Spy Agencies
column24:
First 2 turns had a scheme twist and Masterstrike., glad they both did nothing. Multiple man really helped thin the decks.
I found 2 ways to get infinite attack in this game. Both involve dodge.1 With Yelena have and play Strike and Fade, then dogde Twilight Ops to get it back. Then play strike and fade again. As long as all the cards left in the deck have dodge, it is possible to dodge Twilight Ops over and over. 2 Play Mirage's Haunted by the Demon Bear then just dodge every card. As long as every card has dodge on it this can never end. (Yes my final turn had both scenarios. )
Mirage's rare + Multiple Man + Green Goblin is nuts.

Games 1 and 2 followed mostly the same suit with the left side focusing on Black Bolt and Karnak with hulk buster, while the right had Grey Hulk and No Name focusing on lowering the deck and bumping up attack power.
 On Second game used the 1- cost to search out the Master Strikes and schemes got lucky with no Master Strikes drawn at all and only 2 scheme twists coming out.

When I saw the scheme I had to add the hero designed for it - Psi-Lord. It was an amazing game with many twists and turns, I have KOed 2 of the Master Strikes Preemptively but another two has managed to shown on the exact moment when I had enough generated attack to fight The Hood. Left side focused mainly onPsi-Lord, Brainstorm and a little bit of Hulkbuster mainly for the Draws. The right side was using Karnak and Joe Fixit using the unique combo from Karnak for additional recruit that helped me to fight the vilians and the MM once with recruit thanks to the Joe Fixit cards. Man/ Woman out of time has helped me a lot generating me enough attack to fight something every round and giving me additional options to check the villain decks. I love the scheme and I hope we will see of it more in the future!

The hood is mean, especially epic mode. Photon's ability to count number of discards is amazing alongside all the smash. Lost game 1, and was worried we were on track to lose game 2, but some lucky turns gave us the win!
Absolute insanity. Easily one off the most stress inducing crazy games we've had.  JOE FIXIT rare was amazing

Absolutely brutal. Almost every card was new to me and it really showed in my performance. Got some synergy with 4’s and recruit on the left and War Machine/Hulkbuster on the right, but far too slow and missed out on synergies triggering at the wrong times.

column2: Zombie Green Goblin Unites the Shards
column24:
Game 1 was pretty interesting, Ollie got to play 2 drop Cuckoos & Spidey with a splash of Warpath while Kylah manipulated the HQ with Kitty.  Was fun, but the few 3-drops that Ollie picked up really seemed hurt the synergy. 

Game 2 (this one) - Stellar. Ollie, well he may not have had a ton of VP, but he sure wasn't board. He cycled through the sidekick stack probably twice, playing the sidekick Spidey's with Cuckoos and a few investigate cards. Played 9 or more sidekicks in a turn, typically gained between 4 or 5 sidekicks every few turns. Just silly. Kylah, he got to buy the rest of the 2s to build up the ramp he needed due to the Ult. Spidey rare - Ollie help him end the game by getting the two Prodigy sidekicks he put back up to the top so Kylah could copy the rare, ending the game and clearing what was in the city in a single blow.

One of us went for a straight strength deck while the other went for covert. Ended up gaining a bunch of shards, ignoring the ones on the mastermind with gamora, and using them all to fill back up the supply and kill the mastermind pretty efficiently.
First shuffle i got 3 rares inthe starting hq and conceded. Second shuffle i managed to actually get 2 rares and grinded it down. Henchmen manipulation definitely helped.

I considered a lot of options but went with the double icons of Darkhawk and proxima to control the shards on the mm. Plus they had some class synergy with the other heroes. One side had a lot of covert KO going on and collected shards with Gamora, plus ignored the MMs shards once. The other side had the double icons and bought most of the shards the scheme generated.  

The 2nd attempt went terribly Rise of the living dead is so hard to control depending on the shuffle and timing.

The first game was just an absolute slaughter. ZGG had 10 shards at turn 12 and the game ended before I could get going. Game 2 went much better when I lucked into grabbing Darkhawk's rare early. One side ended up grabbing rares for Darkhawk, Proxima, AND Gamora and just cleaned house. The other side focused on Gamora and Warpath's commons to setup individually big turns to help clear cities and that worked ~moderately well.
Proof you need to ban iceman from competitive
Symbiote Spider Man and Elektra whipped Carnage's behind!!
Iceman is one of my favs

The Symbiote Spider-Man and Elektra deck came out pretty hard. Was only able to get Elektra's uncommon early on, but that was all I needed with all the Spider-Man I was recruiting. The last few turn I was drawing all my deck with up to 29 attack. Carnage never had a chance

Other hand had an okay Moon Knight deck.
Fun heroes plus tons of feast and KO make for some slick decks!
Ready to not play Carnage for awhile - feasting on Rares hurts, even in a win!

Moon Knight and Deadpool with a touch of Emma Frost makes for a solid deck. Elektra and Symbiote Spiderman also worked great together. The HQ did get a little flooded with 3 Sai Blades that I couldn't afford on one side and didn't want on the other side. But the decks worked fine without it

After 2 basically unavoidable early escapes, 2 very powerful decks came to be. Pure synergy, X-Men everywhere. Nearly over-KO’d, but got it just right and started drawing full decks every turn. Carnage was a little annoying, but ultimately couldn’t stand up to Heroes on this day.

what a fun game that was! some moon knight synergy with emma frost and the other side pure low costs (+electra's 6drop) game 1 won in 22 turns and game 2 in 26 but overall i think game 2 will score better cuz of the 0escapes and a bit more VP/bystanders.
Why couldn't this have been a setup I got during the actual tournament?
Never could get that Elektra payoff uncommon.
Wolverine coupled with Elektra built fast ... not quite fast enough for us to prevent 2 escapes, but much faster than the other combos we had in the setup.

I got 3 Twists up front on my first game and thought "Welp this one is in the bag" then I couldn't capitalize on it. It took more turns than my second try. I think I could have done much better. I think I failed to plan for the villains in this game. I picked heroes based on who would do better with KOing cards when I should have picked based on wounds. Not to mention how many turns both runs had where I couldn't fight anyone not even a Sentinel
